'''Darren Burns''' (born 17 May 1974) is an Australian former [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|lk}} for the [[Western Suburbs Magpies]], [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], [[Sydney Roosters]] and the [[Brisbane Broncos]] over his seven-year career in the Australian [[National Rugby League]] competition.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.roosters.com.au/about/past-players/|title=Men's Honour Roll|website=Sydney Roosters|access-date=23 January 2019|archive-date=27 March 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190327090103/https://www.roosters.com.au/about/past-players/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Background==
Burns was born in [[Dalby, Queensland]], and played his junior rugby league for the Dalby Devils.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/news/queensland/gympie/sport/league-passion-labour-of-love-for-devils-stalwart/news-story/f080ee8874bc16eab6f46ff09a0cde5f|title=League passion labour of love for Devils' stalwart|website=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=8 February 2022}}</ref>

==Playing career==
{{BLP unreferenced section|date=September 2024}}
He is best remembered for scoring the first NRL try in 1998, and his part in helping [[Warrington Wolves]] escape relegation in 2002, and in particular his performance in the 29–14 victory over [[Castleford Tigers]] at Wilderspool.
